cities.name: Paris
cities.tagline: Food, art, music and quiet corners — gathered with soul.
cities.bestTime: April–June and September–October. Spring brings blossom and café terraces without the August crush; early autumn is warm, golden and far quieter once the summer crowds leave. Avoid August, when many locals shut up shop and leave the city.

itinerary.title: Classic Paris
itinerary.plan: Morning at the Louvre (book the first slot), then walk the Tuileries down to the Seine. Lunch in the 1st, afternoon at Notre-Dame and the Île de la Cité. End with sunset at the Eiffel Tower and a slow dinner in the 7th.

itinerary.title: Montmartre & the north
itinerary.plan: Climb to Sacré-Cœur early before the crowds, wander the artists' square and back lanes. Afternoon at the Musée d'Orsay for the Impressionists, then an aperitif along Canal Saint-Martin with the after-work crowd.

itinerary.title: Le Marais & the Left Bank
itinerary.plan: Brunch and vintage shopping in the Marais, falafel on Rue des Rosiers. Cross to the Latin Quarter for Shakespeare & Company and the Panthéon, then drinks and live music in Saint-Germain to close the trip.

cities.name: Amsterdam
cities.tagline: Canals, Golden-Age art, brown cafés and long, generous nights.
cities.bestTime: April–May and September. Late April is tulip season and King's Day (27 April) turns the whole city orange — magical but very busy. September has mild weather, golden canal light and thinner crowds. Winters are dark and cold, but the city glows during the Amsterdam Light Festival (Dec–Jan).

itinerary.title: Canals & the old centre
itinerary.plan: Start at Dam Square and the Royal Palace, wander the Nine Streets and the Jordaan, stopping for apple pie at Winkel 43. Take an afternoon canal cruise on a small open boat, then dinner at Moeders and a first night out around Leidseplein (Chicago Social Club or Paradiso).

itinerary.title: Museums & the south
itinerary.plan: Rijksmuseum at opening (straight to the Night Watch), then the Van Gogh Museum and a picnic on Museumplein. Stroll Vondelpark, taste cheese at Reypenaer, then a glam night at Jimmy Woo or a techno session at Shelter across the IJ.

itinerary.title: Local Amsterdam
itinerary.plan: Morning bike ride along the canals to De Pijp and the Albert Cuyp Market for stroopwafels. Afternoon at the A'DAM Lookout swing across the river, sunset photos at the Magere Brug and the Seven Bridges, then a final, easy night dancing at Disco Dolly or Club NYX.

cities.name: Montreal
cities.tagline: European soul, North American energy — bagels, bistros, and all-night beats.
cities.bestTime: Late spring through early fall (May–September) is the sweet spot, when terrasses open, festivals fill the Quartier des Spectacles, and Tam-Tams take over Mount Royal. June and July bring the Jazz Festival and warm patio nights, while September offers golden weather with thinner crowds. Winter is magical but bitterly cold, ideal mainly for skating, the underground city, and Notre-Dame's Aura light show.

itinerary.title: Old Montreal & the Waterfront
itinerary.plan: Start with cobblestones in Vieux-Montréal and the cobalt-and-gold interior of Notre-Dame Basilica, then dig into the city's origins at Pointe-à-Callière. Wander the Old Port, ride La Grande Roue, and unwind with a thermal circuit at the floating Bota Bota spa before dinner and tiki cocktails at Le Mal Nécessaire in nearby Chinatown.

itinerary.title: Plateau, Mile End & Mountain Views
itinerary.plan: Fuel up on hot bagels at St-Viateur and espresso at Café Olimpico in Mile End, then hike Mount Royal to the Kondiaronk Belvedere for the skyline. Stroll Avenue Mont-Royal and the Saint-Laurent murals, grab smoked meat at Schwartz's, and end with craft beer at Dieu du Ciel! or a show at Casa del Popolo.

itinerary.title: Markets, Art & Nightlife
itinerary.plan: Graze through Jean-Talon Market in Little Italy, then explore the Montreal Museum of Fine Arts or immersive art at the PHI Centre. Bike the Lachine Canal to Atwater Market for lunch, and cap the night with natural wine at Vin Mon Lapin or dancing at Stereo's legendary afterhours.

cities.name: London
cities.tagline: Centuries of history, world-class culture, and a pub on every corner.
cities.bestTime: Late spring (May–June) and early autumn (September) are ideal, with long daylight, mild weather, and parks in full bloom or golden colour. Summer is warmest and liveliest but busiest and priciest, while December brings magical Christmas lights and markets despite short, chilly days. Avoid betting on sunshine year-round — pack a light rain jacket whenever you come.

itinerary.title: Classic London Landmarks
itinerary.plan: Start at the Tower of London and walk across Tower Bridge, then ride an Uber Boat along the Thames to Westminster for Big Ben and Westminster Abbey. End with sunset views from the free Sky Garden and an evening pint at Gordon's Wine Bar, London's oldest.

itinerary.title: Markets, Museums & the South Bank
itinerary.plan: Graze through Borough Market for lunch, then cross the Millennium Bridge to Tate Modern and St Paul's Cathedral. Wander the City to photogenic Leadenhall Market and St Dunstan in the East, then cap the night with live jazz at Ronnie Scott's in Soho.

itinerary.title: Local Neighbourhoods & Hidden Corners
itinerary.plan: Browse the beautiful Daunt Books in Marylebone, then head to colourful Notting Hill and Portobello Road. Climb Primrose Hill for the skyline, explore East London's Columbia Road and Brick Lane (grab a salt-beef bagel at Beigel Bake), and finish with cocktails at Shoreditch speakeasy Nightjar.
